steps.label: cause
steps.text: Freshly pressed must contains suspended grape solids before alcoholic fermentation.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Cooling and holding the must allows heavier particles to settle so that clearer juice can be racked away from the sediment.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The must is clarified before fermentation, reducing coarse solids while retaining the level of turbidity intended by the producer.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Desfangado clarifies must before fermentation by settling and separating suspended grape solids.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
